evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC for 4 classifiers with AUROC/AP annotations, diagonal chance line, and prevalence baseline — fully satisfies the brief's scientific requirements.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=none set anywhere in gen_figure.py; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (non-editable bitmap) DejaVu Sans — the matplotlib default font.
evidence.palette: Grey/blue/pink/green palette is colorblind-safe (Paul Tol-style); green reserved for OursModel (directional highlight), not used for red/green direction confusion.
evidence.layout_density: square aspect ratio, tight_layout(pad=1.5), no overlapping labels observed; y-range -0.02 to 1.05 is sensible for both panels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure; OursModel identified as best; legends placed inside axes at lower-right/lower-left which may overlap curves near the origin in the PRC panel.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmap) fonts — not Type 42 TrueType. Text is not selectable or editable. Script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (25 KB), figure.png (101 KB), caption.tex (544 B), gen_figure.py (3.7 KB); bonus figure.svg also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states sample sizes (n_pos≈120, n_neg≈180), names the best method, and describes reference lines, but does not quote the actual AUROC/AP numerical values in the caption text.

overall_strengths: Complete scientific implementation: ROC+PRC curves, per-method AUROC/AP legend annotations, diagonal and prevalence baselines — all brief requirements met.
Colorblind-safe palette with consistent linestyle differentiation across all four methods, and OursModel visually emphasized with thicker solid line.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=none) settings, resulting in non-editable Type 3 bitmap fonts in both the PDF and SVG outputs.
Caption does not include inline numerical AUROC/AP values; a reviewer would expect at least one concrete metric quoted in the caption body rather than only in the legend.
